text.title: `agent-fixable` Triage Gate (Humans Pick, Agents Work) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the task-agent-fixable-triage-gate methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The agent dispatch workflow MUST subscribe only to `issues: types: [labeled]` with an `if: github.event.label.name == 'agent-fixable'` guard (or equivalent in GitLab/Jira). Agents MUST NOT listen on opened/reopened/edited events.
rule.rationale: Making the label expensive to apply and free to remove produced the 34→67% merge-rate jump; same shape works for any coding-agent fleet.

rule.statement: Coding agents MUST NOT apply, modify, or remove the `agent-fixable` label on any issue, including those they are currently working on. A branch-protection or repo-policy rule SHOULD reject label changes from actors matching `*-bot$` or `agent-*`.
rule.rationale: Self-labelling collapses the gate: if the agent can grant itself the precondition, the precondition is no longer a human decision.

rule.statement: Only one label name (`agent-fixable`) MUST be applied by humans/triage. Vendor-specific labels (`agent:devin`, `agent:copilot`) MAY be added by the router AFTER pickup, never before.

rationale.source: Cognition + GitHub Copilot Coding Agent docs

rule.rationale: Keeping the label vendor-neutral lets the router rebalance load without humans relabelling, and lets failed pickup in vendor A fall through to vendor B without triage churn.

rule.statement: If a triage bot applies the label, its tool list MUST NOT include code-write or PR-write capabilities. A passing pre-label checklist MUST exist as a checked artifact (issue comment, ticket field, or audit-log row) before the label lands.
rule.rationale: Conflating triage and coding identities defeats the audit trail; the label loses its meaning as a human-or-equivalent decision.

rule.statement: The dispatch workflow MUST emit a metric per agent pickup recording outcome (merged / abandoned / human-overrode). Merge rate per agent MUST be reviewed weekly; agents below the agreed threshold (typical: 50%) are throttled.
rule.rationale: Without telemetry the gate becomes ceremony; the only way to keep the label allowlist honest is to watch its effect on merge rate over time.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
